message:
fix(monitoring,storage): close global-flags bypass on splunk/datadog/gcs/azure_blob

Same class bug remained on four more packs that used the simple
`<cli>\s+<sub>` shape instead of the structured flag-aware pattern.

- `monitoring/splunk.rs`: `splunk -auth admin:pw remove index main`
  and similar `-uri …` / `-auth …` global flags bypassed every
  rule (remove-index, clean-eventdata, delete-user/role). Fixed the
  3 destructive + 3 safe patterns.

- `monitoring/datadog.rs`: `datadog-ci --config-path=...` between
  `datadog-ci` and subcommand; also `terraform -chdir=./prod destroy
  datadog_*` shape. Fixed the 2 destructive + 2 safe patterns and
  the terraform-destroy-datadog-resource rule.

- `storage/gcs.rs`: `gcloud --project prod storage buckets delete`,
  `gsutil -m -o 'GSUtil:parallel_process_count=4' rb gs://bucket`.
  Fixed all 3 destructive + 7 safe patterns.

- `storage/azure_blob.rs`: `az --subscription prod storage container
  delete`. Fixed all 4 destructive + 12 safe patterns.

All converted to `<cli>\b.*?\b<sub>` form consistent with the wider
sweep. Full suite: 2,153 passed, 0 failed.

files.patch:
